iterations.description: Observation: starting from scratch with no files. Analysis: need baseline pipeline to measure iteration time. Decision: implement ESM2 t30_150M frozen + MLP head, train 2 epochs, submit eval.

Observation: baseline frozen ESM2-150M mean-pooled + MLP head gave spearman_rho=-0.0178 (near random). Training loss decreased from 9.3 to 7.4 in 2 epochs, but performance extremely poor. Analysis: frozen mean pooling over full 735-AA sequence dilutes mutation signals concentrated in positions 561-588. The model cannot capture fitness-relevant mutations. Decision: implement LoRA fine-tuning of ESM2-150M with CLS token representation, train 5 epochs with mixed precision, submit eval to measure improvement and iteration time.

Observation: Iteration 2 failed with TypeError due to peft Lora integration with ESM2 (unexpected keyword argument 'input_ids'). Baseline frozen ESM2 (mean pool) gave spearman_rho=-0.0178, essentially random. Analysis: LoRA wrapping via peft is incompatible with ESM2's forward interface. The cached embeddings from iteration 1 likely contain per-position representations. We can exploit these to focus on the known mutation hotspot (positions 561-588). A small transformer on this 28-AA window can capture epistasis and avoid dilution from the full 735-AA sequence. This approach is fast and compute-efficient, leaving time for many epochs. Decision: implement RegionalTransformer over the mutation region, load cached embeddings (or recompute if missing), train for 50 epochs with random search hyperparams, and submit eval to measure improvement over baseline. Will also compute validation Spearman locally for proxy monitoring.

iterations.description: Observation: Iteration 3 killed (exit code 137) after 7.5 min, likely CPU RAM OOM from attempting to store full 737-length ESM2 embeddings for 31.8k sequences (≈53 GB vs 48 GB RAM). Baseline spearman -0.0178 (random). Analysis: The memory bottleneck was loading full-length embeddings into CPU memory. We only need the 28-AA mutation region (positions 561-588). By slicing embeddings on-the-fly during batch computation and only accumulating the region, we reduce memory to ~2.2 GB. This is critical to make the pipeline work. Decision: Rewrite train.py to compute region-sliced embeddings in a memory-efficient streaming manner, cache them (2.2 GB), train RegionalTransformer for 100 epochs with validation Spearman tracking, and submit eval. Also update predict.py to stream test embeddings similarly.

Analysis: Val Spearman is promising; now need actual test metric to gauge progress and decide next steps. Decision: Submit eval with best checkpoint immediately to get test Spearman, then plan improvements based on gap between val and test.

iterations.description: Observation: test spearman 0.26 vs val 0.78 indicates severe overfitting due to two_vs_many split. Frozen embeddings + small head cannot generalize to higher-order mutants. Analysis: Need a more expressive model that can adapt representations to mutation patterns. Fine-tuning a smaller ESM2 (35M) on full sequences with region pooling can better capture epistasis and generalize. Decision: implement end-to-end fine-tuning of ESM2-12_35M, train for few epochs, submit eval to measure improvement.

iterations.description: Observation: Tokenization failed due to variable sequence lengths (some 736 chars) causing torch.cat error. Test spearman 0.26 vs val 0.78 indicates severe overfitting to low-order mutants. Analysis: Need to fix padding/masking to handle variable-length sequences for ESM2, then test fine-tuning quickly. Also, overfitting suggests we need a model that better captures mutation effects (additive or delta-based). Decision: Implement proper padding/masking for ESM2 tokens, train ESM2-12_35M fine-tuned with region pooling for 5 epochs, submit eval to measure test performance. Then iterate based on new result, possibly incorporating wildtype subtraction to improve generalization.

Observation: Iter 7 failed with ReduceLROnPlateau 'verbose' keyword error. Test spearman still 0.26 with frozen ESM2+head; val 0.78 indicates overfitting. Analysis: Need to fix scheduler (remove verbose) and properly train ESM2-35M fine-tuned version to see if it improves generalization. Tokenization now works (padded tokens cached). Decision: Rewrite train.py with corrected scheduler, add proper mixed‑precision training loop, use cached tokens, train 3 epochs with batch_size=4 to quickly measure test performance and iteration time, then submit eval.

iterations.command_output_summary: Training finished. Best val Spearman: 0.8857 at epoch 5
iterations.description: Observation: Iteration 8 failed with 'mask' argument error again — the ESM2 forward() does not accept a mask parameter as it internally computes attention mask from tokens. Iteration 5 achieved test spearman 0.26 using frozen embeddings + small head; severe overfitting vs val 0.78. Analysis: To improve generalization, we need to fine‑tune ESM2‑35M end‑to‑end. The mask error is easy to fix by removing the mask argument and letting ESM2 handle padding internally. The cached padded tokens are ready. Decision: Fix model.py and predict.py to drop the mask, simplify train.py to use tokens only, train 5 epochs (estimated ~60 min) and submit eval to measure the new test spearman. This will also validate iteration time for future scaling.

iterations.description: Observation: Iteration 9 finished training ESM2-35M fine-tuned with region pooling for 5 epochs, val Spearman improved to 0.8857 (from 0.78 frozen). Training took 3000s (~50 min). No test eval yet for this model. Analysis: Val performance is on low-mutation set; test set (>2 mutations) likely still lower. Need test spearman to quantify generalization gap and decide next steps (more epochs, different architecture, etc.). Decision: Submit eval immediately with best checkpoint to measure test Spearman, then plan improvements based on test performance vs val gap.

iterations.description: Observation: test spearman 0.7322 (best so far) with fine-tuned ESM2-35M and region pooling, but validation spearman 0.8857 indicates overfitting to low-order mutants (two_vs_many split). Training took ~50min for 5 epochs. Analysis: the two_vs_many split requires generalization to higher-order mutants. Subtracting wild-type embeddings (delta approach) forces the model to learn mutation effects rather than absolute fitness, improving generalization. This is a well-known technique in protein fitness prediction. We can implement this by subtracting precomputed wild-type region embedding from each sequence's region embedding before the prediction head. Also, training longer (15 epochs) with early stopping may further improve performance. Decision: modify model and training to incorporate wild-type subtraction, train for 15 epochs (estimated 150min, within 2.8h budget), and submit eval.

Remaining time ~37 min, enough for eval but not for long training. Decision: update predict.py to set weights_only=False, then submit eval using checkpoints/best.pt to measure test performance before further iterations.

iterations.description: Observation: Iteration 12 eval failed due to state_dict mismatch for wt_region_emb key. The checkpoint from training (iter 11) contains the buffer, but when loaded in predict.py the fresh model's state dict lacks it (or reports unexpected). Analysis: The mismatch can be resolved by loading with strict=False and manually computing the wild-type region embedding from the provided WT sequence. This ensures the forward pass uses correct subtraction, matching training behavior. Remaining time ~36 min, insufficient for retraining but enough for eval. Decision: patch predict.py to compute wt_region_emb on the fly, load checkpoint with strict=False, then submit eval using checkpoints/best.pt to obtain test Spearman for the wt-subtraction model.

iterations.description: Observation: iter 13 eval failed with device mismatch (model on CPU, tokens on GPU). Analysis: predict.py computed WT region embedding before moving model to device. Fix is trivial: move model to device before WT forward. Decision: correct predict.py, submit eval using checkpoints/best.pt to measure test spearman for the wt-subtraction model. Remaining time 0.6h is enough for eval (usually <5 min).

iterations.description: Observation: Test spearman improved to 0.8407 after wt-subtraction fine-tuning (iter 11 training). Remaining time ~28 min, enough for short fine-tuning and eval. Analysis: The model likely hasn't fully converged; lowering lr and a few more epochs could further improve generalization. Decision: Resume training from best checkpoint with lr=1e-5 for 2 epochs, save best checkpoint based on val spearman, then submit eval within remaining time.
